search_queries_used: site:entnet.org adult sinusitis update 2025 chronic rhinosinusitis objective confirmation reassessment modifiers polyps
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic antibiotic, steroid, saline, CT, endoscopy, surgery, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Diagnosis requires duration, symptom pattern, examination, and objective evidence interpreted by a clinician.
No investigation, medicine, dose, procedure, treatment, or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with clinician-established or suspected chronic or recurrent rhinosinusitis; children, pregnancy, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, and acute complications require separate pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary or ENT outpatient sinus-symptom follow-up.
UAE_applicability: AAO-HNSF guidance requires UAE imaging, allergy, immune, antimicrobial, prescribing, ENT, and surgical-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: The official AAO-HNSF 2025 update and summary key action statements were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2025 update is current; the official webpage target-age inconsistency is retained as a limitation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact reassessment statement supports interval change, symptom impact, associated or negative features, concerns, and clinician reassessment without declaring treatment failure.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports interval change, persistent or worsening symptom review, clinician reassessment, and documented follow-up without changing treatment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact chronic-sinusitis section supports clinician-performed ENT assessment, reviewed imaging or specialist reports, modifying-factor context, and polyp documentation without interpreting CT or ordering endoscopy.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact symptom-pattern section supports clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions only when documented; it does not label bacterial disease or choose an antibiotic.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting symptom duration and pattern, purulent drainage, nasal obstruction, facial pain or pressure, worsening pattern, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without asserting viral or bacterial diagnosis.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact duration, symptom pattern, smell, obstruction, discharge, facial pressure, dental cause, asthma, immune status, examination, endoscopy, imaging, diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
